name: Classic Green Salsa
recipeIngredient: 25 Whole Tomatillos or 12-15 small green tomatoes
1 Teaspoon Garlic powder or 2 fresh cloves, minced
2 Tablespoons Extra virgin olive oil or 1 tablespoon butter
1 Whole Pequin chili (optional for extra heat)
recipeInstructions:
This salsa, known as Salsa Verde, can be made with either tomatillos or raw green tomatoes. If using green tomatoes, the flavor will change significantly if processed; it may separate during processing but can be fixed by vigorous shaking.
1. Dice the tomatillos or green tomatoes into medium small chunks.
2. Finely chop all remaining vegetables.
3. Juice the lemon (or lime).
4. In a large mixing bowl, gently toss all ingredients with a wooden spoon after adding spices and oil or butter.
5. Transfer the mixture to a serving dish or 1-quart jar and chill for at least one hour before serving. It keeps well in the refrigerator for up to three weeks.
SERVING SUGGESTIONS
Enjoy this salsa with chips and sour cream, or use it on any favorite Mexican dishes. It also works well as a steak or chuck roast sauce. Avoid using it with fish (never tried). If you do, please share your experience.
TIPS:
- To reduce heat intensity, mix in some sour cream to add flavor.
- The level of "heat" can be adjusted by selecting jalapeno peppers with blunt or rounded ends; the more pointed the pepper, the hotter it is.
- Always wear gloves when handling chili peppers to avoid skin absorption and potential irritation to mucus membranes. Capsaicin oil can remain on hands for days even after washing.
